Your Beef Supply Agreement (F27 Cycle) is due for renewal on 30 Jun 2025. You must complete your Environmental Performance Score (EPS) evaluation before your renewal can be submitted.

Environmental Performance Score (EPS)

Required

Your EPS measures year-on-year improvement in emissions intensity (kg CO₂-e per kg HSCW). Completing this section is mandatory — renewal submission is blocked until your EPS is on file.

Top-quartile suppliers qualify for preferential shelf placement in the Woolworths premium beef range. You will also receive an anonymised benchmark showing how your score compares across all Woolworths beef suppliers.

Estimated time to complete: under 20 minutes.

Renewal Submission

Complete all required steps above to unlock submission.

Sustainability Engagement Meeting

F27 Renewal Cycle  ·  Rocky Beef  ·  Bottom-Quartile Follow-Up

Sustainability Engagement Meeting Agenda Bottom-Quartile Supplier
Supplier
Rocky Beef
Contract
Beef Supply Agreement
Meeting Date
Please check your email
Prepared by
Woolworths Sustainability Team

Purpose: Collaborate on emissions reduction and agree an improvement target for the F28 EPS.

1
Review of Current Emissions Intensity Data
  • Review F26 and F27 emissions intensity results
  • Discuss drivers of change and data sources (MLA SSP)
  • Identify key opportunities and challenges
Outcome: Shared understanding of performance and data
2
Overview of Emissions-Reduction Support (MLA CN30 Program)
  • Program options and funding available through MLA's CN30
  • Practical support: advisory, tools, and on-farm actions
  • Case studies from beef producers
Outcome: Agreed support pathway and next steps
3
Agree Improvement Target for F28 (Baseline for Next EPS)
  • Set a measurable emissions intensity target for F28
  • Confirm actions and milestones to achieve the target
  • Record agreed target in the portal
Outcome: Committed target recorded and locked for F28 EPS

Agreed target will be used as the official baseline input for the F28 EPS evaluation.

Top-Quartile Outcome

Congratulations, Rocky Beef!

Your EPS qualifies your product for preferential placement in Woolworths' premium beef range for F27.

Thank you for your leadership in reducing emissions and supporting a more sustainable beef industry.

Document verification is still pending. Our team will reach out to you if any clarification is required.
Bottom-Quartile Outcome

Rocky Beef, we need to work together.

Your EPS has not improved.

A sustainability engagement meeting has been scheduled before your F28 renewal is confirmed.

Our team will be in touch with meeting details and preparation materials.

EPS Evaluation

F27 Renewal Cycle  ·  Rocky Beef  ·  Environmental Performance Score

Welcome to your EPS Evaluation

The Environmental Performance Score (EPS) is a disclosure initiative embedded in Woolworths' annual supplier contract renewal process. You are simply required to report your emissions intensity figures and submit supporting documentation — no specific reduction technology is mandated. Your data will be taken into consideration across all of your supply agreements. Top-quartile performers qualify for preferential shelf placement in the Woolworths premium beef range. Bottom-quartile suppliers who do not improve across two consecutive cycles will be invited to a sustainability engagement meeting before renewal is confirmed.

Estimated time Under 20 minutes
Environmental Performance Score (EPS)
Data source: MLA Supplier Sustainability Program

Your prior-year emissions intensity has been pre-populated.

Emissions Intensity (kg CO₂-e / kg HSCW)

Prior Year (F26)
Auto-populated from MLA

kg CO₂-e / kg HSCW

Has your emissions intensity improved this year?

Improvement: 10.3%
Great work
Supporting Documents

Please submit the following documents to support your EPS submission. All documents must be current (within the last 12 months). Required documents must be uploaded before submission.

MLA Supplier Sustainability Program (SSP) Report
Your annual SSP report confirming emissions intensity data from Meat & Livestock Australia.
Required
Click to upload
PDF or XLSX
Livestock Production Records
Head count, liveweight, and HSCW data for the reporting year to verify your emissions denominator.
Required
Click to upload
PDF or XLSX
On-Farm Emissions Assessment Report
An independent or self-assessed on-farm emissions audit covering enteric fermentation, manure, and land use.
Required
Click to upload
PDF or XLSX
National Greenhouse & Energy Reporting (NGER) Statement
Required if your operation meets NGER reporting thresholds under the Clean Energy Regulator.
If applicable
Click to upload
PDF or XLSX
Third-Party Verification Certificate
Optional accreditation from a recognised body (e.g. Carbon Market Institute, AUS-MEAT) supporting your emissions claim.
If applicable
Click to upload
PDF or XLSX
